header.title: UI Components
header.subtitle: Component library and design system reference for the NexDash template. Browse, preview, and copy code snippets for every component.

feedback.label: Feedback
feedback.description: Toasts, empty states, error states, skeletons, spinner

header.title: Toasts
header.subtitle: Non-blocking notifications that appear in the bottom-right corner and auto-dismiss after a configurable delay. Use toasts for transient feedback: confirmations, errors, and status updates. Avoid them for critical information that requires user action — use a dialog instead.

variants.title: Variants
variants.description: Four semantic variants, each with a distinct color and icon.

withDescription.title: With description
withDescription.description: An optional second line for additional context.

withAction.title: With action
withAction.description: An inline button that lets the user undo or take a follow-up action.

persistent.title: Persistent
persistent.description: Pass duration: 0 to disable auto-dismiss. The user must close it manually.

queue.title: Queue overflow
queue.description: The queue is capped at 5 visible toasts. Older ones are removed (FIFO) when the limit is exceeded.

api.title: API
api.description: Options accepted by all toast.* methods.

props.title: Main message shown in the toast.
props.description: Optional secondary line below the title.
props.duration: Auto-dismiss time in ms. Pass 0 for persistent.
props.action: Inline action button rendered below the title.

methods.error: Show a red error toast (6 s default).
methods.warning: Show an amber warning toast (5 s default).
methods.dismiss: Start exit animation for a specific toast.
methods.clear: Immediately remove all toasts from the queue.

header.title: Empty states
header.subtitle: Shown when a list or content area has no items to display. An empty state sets expectations, explains why nothing is here, and gives the user a clear path forward.

variants.title: Variants
variants.description: Three built-in illustrations for the most common empty-state contexts.

withDescription.title: With description
withDescription.description: Add context to explain why the state is empty and what the user can do.

withAction.title: With action
withAction.description: A primary CTA gives the user a direct path to populate the empty list.

withTwoActions.title: With two actions
withTwoActions.description: A secondary action offers an alternative path without competing with the primary CTA.

customIcon.title: Custom icon
customIcon.description: Pass any React node as icon to override the built-in illustration.

illustrations.title: Illustrations
illustrations.description: The three illustration components can be imported and used standalone.

withDescription.title: Description
withDescription.stateDescription: Reports you create will appear here. Start by choosing a template.

withAction.title: Single action
withAction.stateDescription: Invite your team to get started.

withTwoActions.title: Primary + secondary
withTwoActions.stateDescription: Upload a CSV or connect to a data source.

customIcon.title: Lucide icon
customIcon.stateDescription: Drag and drop files, or click to browse.

props.title: Primary heading text.
props.description: Optional supporting sentence below the title.
props.icon: Custom illustration or icon. Overrides variant.
props.secondaryAction: Secondary CTA rendered next to action.
props.className: Extra classes applied to the container.

header.title: Error states
header.subtitle: Shown when a data fetch, mutation, or render fails. Error states replace or augment the content area so the user understands something went wrong and knows how to recover.

default.title: Default
default.description: Centered layout with icon, title, description, and optional retry button. Use inside a card or as a section replacement.

compact.title: Compact
compact.description: Single-line format for inline use — inside table rows, list items, or small panels.

customCopy.title: Custom copy
customCopy.description: Override the default title and description to match the failing context.

devDetails.title: Dev details
devDetails.description: Pass the Error object to expose a collapsible technical detail block in development. Hidden in production.

routeLevel.title: Route-level error
routeLevel.description: Next.js error.tsx boundaries receive { error, reset } props. Use EmptyState variant="error" paired with useRouter for navigation recovery.

customCopy.title: Custom message
customCopy.stateDescription: The analytics service is temporarily unavailable.

props.title: Error heading.
props.onRetry: If provided, renders a retry button.
props.error: Exposes technical details in dev mode.
props.size: "compact" collapses to a single-line inline format.
props.className: Extra classes applied to the container.

header.title: Skeletons
header.subtitle: Placeholder animations shown while data is loading. Skeletons reduce perceived latency by mirroring the shape of the real content. Always match the skeleton dimensions to the real component to avoid layout shift (CLS).

primitives.title: Primitives — SkeletonBase
primitives.description: Low-level building block with five preset shapes. Use for ad-hoc placeholders.

kpiCard.title: KpiCardSkeleton
kpiCard.description: Matches the KpiCard component shape. Drop in as a direct loading replacement.

chart.title: ChartSkeleton
chart.description: Four chart types: bar, line, area, donut. Set height to match the real chart container.

table.title: TableSkeleton
table.description: Mimics a data table with configurable row and column counts.

list.title: ListSkeleton
list.description: Mimics an activity feed or avatar list.

form.title: FormSkeleton
form.description: Stacked label + input pairs with an optional submit button.

userCard.title: UserCardSkeleton
userCard.description: Avatar + name/email line + status badge. Matches the user row pattern.

comparison.title: Skeleton vs real
comparison.description: Toggle between the skeleton and a representative real component to verify the size match.

table.showHeader: Whether to render the header row.

header.title: Sidebar patterns
header.subtitle: The sidebar is data-driven — all items are defined in a single config file. Supports sections with titles, direct links, and collapsable groups up to 2 levels deep.

structure.title: Section structure
structure.description: Sidebar items are organised in three tiers. The maximum depth is 2 — groups cannot contain other groups.

section.description: Visual grouping with an optional UPPERCASE title. Rendered with a divider between sections.

link.description: Direct navigation item with optional icon, badge, and count.

group.description: Collapsable row with a chevron. Opens to reveal child SidebarLinks.

child.description: Child of a SidebarGroup. Shown indented with no icon. A vertical guide line on the left marks the group hierarchy.

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.description: The full sidebarConfig shows all features: section with title, direct link, group with children, badge.

persistence.title: Persistence behaviour
persistence.description: The sidebar uses a two-signal approach: the Zustand store (persisted) tracks which groups the user has explicitly opened, and the active route always expands its containing group.

navigateInto.result: Reports group auto-expands. Saved in store.

navigateAway.result: Reports group stays open (still in store).

navigateBack.result: Reports group re-expands (route change adds to store).

openAnother.scenario: User opens another group while in /reports

refresh.result: Store rehydrates from localStorage. Groups that were open remain open.

persistence.footnote: Store key: nexdash-sidebar in localStorage. Implemented in src/store/sidebar.store.ts.

collapsed.title: Collapsed mode
collapsed.description: When the sidebar is collapsed to icon-only width (w-16), groups show a hover popover instead of expanding inline.

rows.link: Icon only. Tooltip with label on hover (side="right").
rows.group: Icon only. Hover shows SidebarPopover to the right — a floating panel with group title and all child links.
rows.popover: Portal to document.body. Appears on mouseEnter/focus, stays open while cursor is on it (150ms close delay). Escape closes and returns focus to trigger.

addingGroup.title: Adding a collapsable group
addingGroup.description: Two-step process: add routes, then add the group to sidebarConfig.
addingGroup.footnote: The group ID is computed as '{sectionId}.{kebab-label}' — e.g. workspace.projects. This ID is what gets stored in expandedGroups.

subnav.title: Alternative: in-page sub-navigation
subnav.description: If your section has many sub-pages and you don't want to expand the main sidebar, you can use the UiSubnav pattern as in-page secondary navigation. This was the original approach of /ui before being replaced by the main sidebar's collapsible groups.
subnav.demoDescription: Currently active based on the page you're on. In production, it would drive navigation within a section.
subnav.whenToUse: When to use UiSubnav vs sidebar sections: Use sidebar sections (this page) when the items belong in the primary navigation. Use UiSubnav when the section is supplementary (e.g. an admin-only area) and adding it to the main sidebar would distract from the core product navigation.

section.id: Unique section identifier. Used as React key and for group IDs.
section.title: Label shown in uppercase above section items.
section.items: Array of SidebarLink or SidebarGroup items.

link.type: Discriminant — identifies this as a direct navigation item.
link.label: Text shown in the nav item.
link.href: Route target. Must be in routes.ts.
link.icon: Icon component (not JSX). Shown when sidebar is expanded.
link.badge: Small pill badge rendered to the right of the label.
link.count: Numeric value rendered to the right (monospace).
link.disabled: Renders as non-interactive with reduced opacity.

group.type: Discriminant — identifies this as a collapsable group.
group.label: Group heading and popover title.
group.icon: Icon shown on the group header (and in collapsed popover).
group.children: Child links (max depth 2 — no nested groups).
group.defaultOpen: Pre-expand on first render (before any user interaction).

header.title: Language Switcher
header.subtitle: Switches the active locale and persists it in the NEXT_LOCALE cookie. Locale change uses router.replace so it does not add a browser history entry. Two variants: compact for the topbar and full for settings pages.

compact.title: Compact
compact.description: Dropdown button showing the current locale flag and code. Used in the topbar, between Search and the notification bell.

full.title: Full
full.description: Pill buttons listing all available locales. Used in the Settings → Appearance → Language section.

hook.title: useLocaleSwitch hook
hook.description: The underlying hook. Use it directly if you need to build a custom switcher UI.

props.variant: "compact" renders a dropdown button for topbar use. "full" renders pill buttons for settings pages.
props.className: Extra classes applied to the container.

localizationNote.title: Localization
localizationNote.description: All visible strings should be wrapped with useTranslations from next-intl. The snippets above show literal strings for clarity, but in production code use t() to support multiple languages.
